zh.lifeSummary: Joan de Bassett於大約1324年出生在英國斯塔福爾黑阿德爾。她於從1337年到1338年與Richard Hill在英國戴文艾可謝特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1413年在英國斯塔福爾享年91歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bassett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Old French basset ‘of low stature’, a diminutive of basse ‘low, short’, either a nickname for a short person or a status name for someone of humble origins.
Altered form of French Bessette 1 or Besset (see Bessette 2).

History: William Bassett (c. 1598–1667) came to Plymouth, MA, from Kent, England, in the 1620s; c. 1650 he moved to Duxbury and subesequently to Bridgewater. He had many prominent descendants, among them one of the earliest families on Martha's Vineyard. — The surname Bassett of French origin (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Bessette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Armand, Adrien, Normand, Alphonse, Lucien, Cecile, Herve, Jacques, Laurent, Aime.

French: topographic name designating a small wood of birches, from Occitan bès ‘birch tree’ (see Besse ), or a habitational name from (La) Bessette, the name of several places in the southern part of France.
Alternatively, an altered form of French Besset: topographic name, a cognate of 1 above. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Joan de Bassett was born about 1324, in Cheadle, Staffordshire, England. She married Richard Hill from 1337 to 1338, in Exeter, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1413, in Staffordshire, England, at the age of 91, and was buried in Church, Lancashire, England.

Elle est décédée environ 1413, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 91 ans, et a été enterrée à Church, Lancashire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Bassettは約1324年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアチェアドルで生まれた。から1337年まで1338年にイギリスデボンエクセターでRichard Hillと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1413年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアで91歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアチャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Bassett(은)는 대략 1324년 영국 스태퍼드실르 체아들일에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1337년 부터 1338년 까지 영국 데폰 세터에서 Richard Hill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1413년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 91세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan de Bassett родилась около 1324 г. в местности чидл, стаффордшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Hill с 1337 по 1338 в местности эксетир, девон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1413 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 91 год и была похоронена в местности чурч, ланкашир, Англия.
